question.text: IFSC at GIFT City, Gandhinagar is regulated by which authority?

answer.text: International Financial Services Centres Authority (IFSCA), established in 2020
Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) exclusively
A joint committee of SEBI, RBI, and IRDAI

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The IFSC at GIFT City is governed by the International Financial Services Centres Authority (IFSCA), a unified statutory authority established in 2020. IFSCA regulates all financial products, services, and institutions in the IFSC. The IFSC is treated as a deemed foreign territory dealing in foreign currency. **The Trap:** Before IFSCA was established, different regulators (SEBI, RBI, IRDAI, PFRDA) regulated their respective domains in the IFSC. IFSCA unified this regulation. **Cross-Exam Context:** IFSCA-01 exam specifically covers AML/CFT in the IFSC context. Series XII covers IFSC as part of market infrastructure. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** IFSCA = one regulator for all financial services in IFSC. Key benefits: 10-year (now 20-year as per Union Budget 2026) tax holiday, no STT, no capital gains tax, no GST on offshore services.
